experience.title: Full-Stack Engineer (Adobe Extensions)
bullets: Shipped production <<React>> Adobe After Effects & Premiere Pro extensions, including Vanish BG (real-time AI background removal) running ML inference inside a sandboxed CEP environment and distributed via aescripts.com.
Designed a <<Node.js>> sidecar architecture to bypass CEP runtime limitations, using local HTTP IPC and streaming inference results back to the UI in real time.
Built a Premiere Pro TTS extension with local <<ONNX>> inference, delivering an end-to-end pipeline from text input to rendered timeline audio clips.
Collaborated asynchronously with designers and stakeholders using agile workflows to deliver production-ready tools.

experience.title: Full-Stack Engineer
bullets: Built a full-stack tourism booking platform with <<Next.js>> and <<TypeScript>>, implementing a <<Node.js>> API with type-safe <<PostgreSQL>> access via <<TypeORM>>.
Drove API performance using <<PostgreSQL>> indexing and <<EXPLAIN ANALYZE>>, achieving sub-100ms response times under concurrent load.
Integrated Paymob payments with <<idempotent>> transaction handling and webhook verification, reducing checkout friction by 40%.
Improved SEO and Core Web Vitals to 95+ Lighthouse via <<Next.js SSR>>, image optimization, and lazy loading.

education.degree: Complete SQL Databases Bootcamp: Zero to Mastery
Master the Coding Interview: Data Structures & Algorithms

projects.name: Medical CRM — AI-Enhanced Prescription Platform
highlights: Built a full-stack CRM for diagnoses, prescriptions, and patient records using <<React>>, <<Node.js>>, and <<PostgreSQL>>.
Implemented secure auth with <<JWT>> access/refresh token rotation and <<RBAC>> across three permission levels.
Designed drug interaction validation across 500+ medications using graph-based <<PostgreSQL>> lookups.
Optimized dashboard queries for 10K+ monthly records using composite indexes and window functions.

projects.name: RootCluster — AI-Powered Log Analysis Platform
projects.techStack: Node.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL (pgvector), Redis, BullMQ, ONNX, AWS ECS Fargate, Docker, GitHub Actions
highlights: Designed and deployed a distributed, <<event-driven>> log ingestion pipeline on <<AWS ECS Fargate>> using <<BullMQ>> and <<Redis>>, with <<PostgreSQL (pgvector)>> similarity search, processing 1M+ events/hour with sub-second latency.
Implemented robust <<async processing>> with producer-consumer queues, configurable worker concurrency, and priority queues for urgent events vs. backfills.
Built <<dead-letter queues>> with retry policies (exponential backoff, max attempts) to isolate malformed entries without blocking the pipeline.
Designed <<idempotent>> processing using Redis dedup keys and at-least-once delivery semantics via job completion callbacks.
Reduced <<ONNX>> embedding time from 20+ minutes to under 1 minute via batch processing, Redis caching, and worker autoscaling.
Set up CI/CD using <<GitHub Actions>> with OIDC, Docker image publishing, and AWS integration for secrets and deployments.
